name: Claude Williams
abstract: Claude "Fiddler" Williams (February 22, 1908 – April 25, 2004) was an American jazz violinist and guitarist who recorded and performed into his 90s. He was the first guitarist to record with Count Basie and the first musician to be inducted into the Oklahoma Jazz Hall of Fame.

interviews.title: Claude Williams interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

concerts.label: Folk Masters: Masters of the Folk Violin
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Eastwood After Hours - A Night of Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: Oh no, you had Claude Williams? Yes, that's right. Yes, we did have Claude. But see, he didn't really play the in/Reno with us, but he did go with us to New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Genevieve Rose
about_quotes.evidence: we were playing for Claude Williams’ 90th birthday... with Bucky Pizzarelli also, he was playing guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Genevieve Rose interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: Well, truthfully, the first time I worked with Claude Williams, it was about 1969 or 1970.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: So we had him in that band, in the Holder band, and Claude Williams was in that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: The Blue Devils. Well, he got Walter Page on bass... I watched them come in night by night... I was the unhired regular drummer in the band until Jo got there

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I’m with him, and Panama Francis and Claude Williams, and I’m just like, said to myself, you should really take note of this and appreciate it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
